Title: An HDG11-mediated transcriptional network regulates 1-deoxynojirimycin biosynthesis in mulberry

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Notable pharmacological properties; clinically approved mulberry-derived DNJ is used for diabetes treatment
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Buds and roots; higher in young leaves than mature leaves; undetectable in callus
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Terminal DNJ-forming step in the proposed glucose-derived DNJ route
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes the conversion of ADM to DNJ
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key control point; central gene in DNJ biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes DNJ accumulation; reduced expression lowers DNJ and increased expression elevates DNJ
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: In vitro enzymatic assay; overexpression; RNA interference/RNAi; VIGS-linked expression analysis; RT-qPCR; metabolite profiling by LC-ESI-MS/MS/UHPLC-MS
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Predominantly expressed in buds; strongly correlated with DNJ accumulation across tissues; negligible in roots; low/absent in callus
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: In M. alba leaves, MaGutB1 is induced by ABA and tZR treatment
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Tissue type/developmental stage; ABA treatment; trans-zeatin riboside (tZR) treatment
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MnGutB1 is a key DNJ biosynthetic gene identified in the DNJ-associated MEblue module. 2. Previous in vitro assays validated that it converts ADM to DNJ. 3. Its expression is activated directly by ZFHD6, TCP14, and ATHB40, and indirectly by HDG11. 4. MnGutB1 transcript abundance closely matches tissue-specific DNJ accumulation.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq, GSA: CRA029611; WGCNA; targeted phytohormone metabolomics
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Moderate induction of MaGutB1 does not proportionally increase DNJ, possibly because DNJ is converted into derivatives and/or constrained by feedback control
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Ortholog/related gene MaGutB1 was examined in Morus alba cv. Guiyou12 for hormone response and hairy root overexpression assays. Root DNJ accumulation may partly reflect transport/storage rather than local biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Prime metabolic engineering target for increasing DNJ; likely useful in gene stacking with positive TFs (HDG11, ZFHD6, TCP14, ATHB40) and with ABA/tZR induction strategies; caution that DNJ derivative formation and feedback regulation may limit gains.

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Notable pharmacological properties; mulberry-derived DNJ is used for diabetes treatment
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Buds and roots; higher in young leaves
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Indirect transcriptional activation; upstream hierarchical activation; protein complex formation with partner TFs
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates expression of ZFHD6, TCP14, and ATHB40, thereby indirectly promoting MnGutB1 expression
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: ABA; trans-zeatin riboside (tZR); correlated also with I3C, IAA, SA, and JA across tissues
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: No direct loop proven; discussion hypothesizes possible negative feedback limiting DNJ accumulation under hormone induction
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Forms binary transcriptional complexes with TCP14 and ATHB40; no interaction with ZFHD6; does not bridge TCP14 and ATHB40 into a ternary complex
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly expressed in buds; strongly positively correlated with MnGutB1 and DNJ across tissues (Pearson's ρ = 0.97)
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Induced in M. alba cv. Guiyou12 leaves within 8 h after ABA or tZR treatment
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: 35S promoter for subcellular localization constructs; pZYGC overexpression construct in hairy roots; VIGS via 2mDNA1-based system
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: WGCNA/co-expression; RT-qPCR; dual-luciferase assay; VIGS; hairy root overexpression; Y2H; BiFC; Y3H; DNJ metabolite profiling
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Positive regulator that promotes DNJ accumulation indirectly; silencing reduces MnGutB1 and DNJ, overexpression increases them
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: HDG11-centered upstream regulator integrating hormone and developmental signals; strongest TF correlation with DNJ and MnGutB1
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. HDG11 expression most strongly correlates with MnGutB1 and DNJ accumulation. 2. It does not bind or directly activate the MnGutB1 promoter. 3. HDG11 upregulates ZFHD6, TCP14, and ATHB40, which directly activate MnGutB1. 4. HDG11 forms binary complexes with TCP14 and ATHB40. 5. ABA and tZR induce HDG11 expression together with increased DNJ.
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Tissue/developmental stage; ABA foliar spray; tZR foliar spray
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq, GSA: CRA029611; WGCNA; targeted phytohormone metabolomics
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Hormone-induced activation yielded only limited DNJ increase, suggesting derivative conversion and/or feedback buffering; no growth penalty reported
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Acts upstream of direct MnGutB1 activators; central node of the proposed transcriptional hierarchy regulating tissue-specific DNJ biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Top regulatory target for engineering DNJ production; could be combined with direct activators or hormone-based induction, but optimization is needed because downstream metabolic buffering may cap DNJ gains.

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Notable pharmacological properties; mulberry-derived DNJ is used for diabetes treatment
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Buds and roots; higher in young leaves
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Direct transcriptional activation through promoter binding
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Directly binds the ZF-HD-1 motif in the MnGutB1 promoter and activates MnGutB1 transcription
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: No interaction detected with HDG11, TCP14, or ATHB40
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Elevated in buds and young leaves; reduced along leaf maturation axis; downregulated in callus; positively correlated with MnGutB1 (ρ = 0.72)
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Upregulated in HDG11-overexpressing lines and downregulated in HDG11-silenced lines
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: 35S promoter for localization constructs; pZYGC overexpression construct in hairy roots; VIGS via 2mDNA1-based system
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RT-qPCR; dual-luciferase assay; Y1H; EMSA; VIGS; hairy root overexpression; DNJ metabolite profiling
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Positive regulator that elevates MnGutB1 expression and DNJ accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Direct activator of the key biosynthetic gene MnGutB1
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ZFHD6 is a bud-enriched candidate TF from the DNJ-associated module. 2. It directly binds the ZF-HD-1 cis-element in the MnGutB1 promoter. 3. Silencing decreases MnGutB1 expression and DNJ content, while overexpression increases them. 4. Its expression is positively controlled by HDG11.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Acts independently of HDG11 physical interaction despite being transcriptionally downstream of HDG11
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful direct activator for DNJ enhancement, especially in combination with MnGutB1 or HDG11-centered regulatory engineering.

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Notable pharmacological properties; mulberry-derived DNJ is used for diabetes treatment
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Buds and roots; higher in young leaves
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Direct transcriptional activation through promoter binding; binary complex formation with HDG11
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Directly binds the TCP-1 motif in the MnGutB1 promoter and activates MnGutB1 transcription
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Forms binary complex with HDG11; no TCP14-ATHB40 interaction and no HDG11-mediated ternary complex detected
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Elevated in buds and young leaves; reduced with leaf maturation; positively correlated with MnGutB1 (ρ = 0.85)
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Upregulated in HDG11-overexpressing lines and downregulated in HDG11-silenced lines
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: 35S promoter for localization constructs; pZYGC overexpression construct in hairy roots; VIGS via 2mDNA1-based system
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RT-qPCR; dual-luciferase assay; Y1H; EMSA; VIGS; hairy root overexpression; Y2H; BiFC; Y3H; DNJ metabolite profiling
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Positive regulator that increases MnGutB1 expression and DNJ accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Direct activator of MnGutB1 and key component of the HDG11-centered network
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TCP14 was identified from the DNJ-associated module and strongly correlates with MnGutB1. 2. It directly binds the TCP-1 site in the MnGutB1 promoter and activates transcription. 3. Silencing lowers DNJ and MnGutB1, while overexpression elevates both. 4. TCP14 interacts with HDG11 in the nucleus. 5. No TCP14-ATHB40 interaction or ternary HDG11 bridge was detected.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: HDG11 may modulate TCP14 function both transcriptionally and via protein interaction
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong direct activator suitable for stacking with HDG11 and MnGutB1 to increase DNJ biosynthesis.

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Notable pharmacological properties; mulberry-derived DNJ is used for diabetes treatment
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Buds and roots; higher in young leaves
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Direct transcriptional activation through promoter binding; binary complex formation with HDG11
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Directly binds the HD-ZIP-1 motif in the MnGutB1 promoter and activates MnGutB1 transcription
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Forms binary complex with HDG11; no ATHB40-TCP14 interaction and no HDG11-mediated ternary complex detected
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Elevated in buds and young leaves; reduced with leaf maturation; moderately correlated with MnGutB1 (ρ = 0.64)
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Upregulated in HDG11-overexpressing lines and downregulated in HDG11-silenced lines
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: 35S promoter for localization constructs; pZYGC overexpression construct in hairy roots; VIGS via 2mDNA1-based system
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RT-qPCR; dual-luciferase assay; Y1H; EMSA; VIGS; hairy root overexpression; Y2H; BiFC; Y3H; DNJ metabolite profiling
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Positive regulator that increases MnGutB1 expression and DNJ accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Direct activator of MnGutB1 within the HDG11-centered network
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ATHB40 is a DNJ-associated TF with bud-preferential expression. 2. It directly binds the HD-ZIP-1 element in the MnGutB1 promoter and activates transcription. 3. Silencing reduces MnGutB1 expression and DNJ, while overexpression raises both. 4. ATHB40 interacts with HDG11 in the nucleus. 5. No ternary complex with TCP14 via HDG11 was supported.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Moderate correlation with MnGutB1 but clear functional support as a direct activator
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate TF for DNJ metabolic engineering, especially in combination with HDG11 and MnGutB1.
